sanskrit_devanagari: अनन्यचेता: सततं यो मां स्मरति नित्यश: |तस्याहं सुलभ: पार्थ नित्ययुक्तस्य योगिन: || 14||
transliteration: ananya-chetāḥ satataṁ yo māṁ smarati nityaśhaḥtasyāhaṁ sulabhaḥ pārtha nitya-yuktasya yoginaḥ
translation: BG 8.14: O Parth, for those yogis who always think of Me with exclusive devotion, I am easily attainable because of their constant absorption in Me.
commentary_excerpt: In the previous verse, Shree Krishna had explained the process of meditation as per ashtanga yoga sadhana to focus on the formless God, devoid of any attributes. However, this practice is not only difficult but also very bland. Therefore, He suggests an alternative method, that is, to meditate upon the personal forms of God such as Ram, Krishna, Shiv, Vishnu, etc., including their Names, Forms, Virtues, Pastimes, Abodes, and Associates.
